title: Stash Internals, Partial Work, and Portable Patches · Collaboration Transfer
summary: An independent maintainer receives a different graph, repository policy, and failure constraint and must transfer stash internals, partial work, and portable patches into a new work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.
Use pathspec, patch, staged, keep-index, include-untracked, and all controls while previewing secret and ignored-file consequences.
Choose apply, pop, branch, store, create, export, or import from collision risk, portability, and retention needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Case G12 is a lab in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-wip-recovery-policy-x65237" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Wip Recovery Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-wip-recovery-policy-x65237
# scenario-key: trace-x65237 probe-73256x boundary-5237x6 owner-x65237x fault-x65237z
# before-ref: main=a11cex6 candidate=b00b137
# hypothesis: only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible
git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip
# expected: the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths
# observation: policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection
# changed-case: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes
# after-ref: main=a11cex6 candidate=c0ffee7
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash
# transfer-limit: inspect real ignored and untracked paths because stash can store sensitive bytes in Git objects
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Gita Wip Recovery Policy structural check is only one gate. work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash. Competency probe: Create named durable refs or encrypted external backups for important work, verify restoration on a changed base, and expire temporary objects deliberately.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A stash is an appropriate durable backup and collaboration channel.

stimulus.title: Gita Wip Recovery Policy failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A stash is an appropriate durable backup and collaboration channel.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create named durable refs or encrypted external backups for important work, verify restoration on a changed base, and expire temporary objects deliberately.

options.text: Keep the plausible Gita Wip Recovery Policy output and hide the violated work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Gita Wip Recovery Policy without its stated preconditions, omit the work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Wip Recovery Policy for work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Gita Wip Recovery Policy condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the lab evidence note for Gita Wip Recovery Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G21 is a lab in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash. Competency probe: Create named durable refs or encrypted external backups for important work, verify restoration on a changed base, and expire temporary objects deliberately.
steps.why: Retrieving Gita Wip Recovery Policy through the concrete work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create named durable refs or encrypted external backups for important work, verify restoration on a changed base, and expire temporary objects deliberately.
